story:
A nearly empty Italian restaurant on a warm night. Zoe and Ben sit outside while Jess is inside with the baby. The air is charged: legs brush, his hand finds her knee, their words circle like a dance. They talk travel—“How long will you be out of town?” “Three days… early flight Tuesday… Chicago,” he says. She teases; he traces a drop of water at her collarbone. He thinks about what it would mean to end this relationship. She rolls an ice cube over her lips, along her wrist, down the line of her dress. The moment tightens.

“Sorry that took so long!” Jess returns, kisses Ben’s cheek, settles GiGi. The spell cracks. “Perks of marrying a successful salesman—he’s always on the move,” Zoe says. “I knew what I was signing up for,” Jess laughs. “Good thing I have you when he’s gone.” “Girls’ night,” Zoe answers. Ben and Zoe risk a glance. It was getting uncomfortable.
implausible_knowledge: He thinks about what it would mean to end this relationship.
question: As they stand to say goodnight at the end of the dinner, what will Zoe most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Keep it strictly friendly, say a quick general goodbye to both, and leave without seeking a private moment.
answer_without_knowledge: Linger until Jess is busy with the baby and quietly tell Ben to text her from Chicago.
